steps.n: This is a special word that comes before verbs to emphasize that something definitely happened. Here it emphasizes that Allah definitely heard.
Connected the root letters together with vowels. This shows that someone (he) did the action of hearing in the past. In this ayah, he (Allah) heard someone's speech (قَوْلَ).
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of يَسْمَعُ (hears) in this ayah.
Changed from action to the thing itself (like changing 'speak' to 'speech'). This word is what Allah heard (object of سَمِعَ - He heard).
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being heard
Basic form of the feminine relative pronoun
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Final spelling adjustments: 1) ا becomes ٱ at start of word, 2) shadda added on ل, 3) ي becomes ى at end. This word refers to the woman who is arguing.

steps.m: to argue or debate back and forth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding ا shows this is an argument between two people
تُ at start shows this is about 'she' doing the action

steps.m: she argues with you (subject of سَمِعَ)
steps.n: Added ك at end to show who she's arguing with. This word is the subject of سَمِعَ (heard) in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows what something is about or where it happens. In this ayah, it connects to the word زَوْجِهَا (her husband) to show what the woman was arguing about.

Adding ت in the middle shows she is doing the action to/for herself
The و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it in the verse
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects with the word اللَّهِ to show the complaint is being directed 'to Allah'.

Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word before it (إِلَى meaning 'to'). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

Added shadda on ل to show it is pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (hearing) in this sentence

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject doing something)

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject doing something)

steps.m: he hears (Allah hears in this ayah)

steps.m: to go back and forth, exchange

Added alif to show two people talking back and forth

Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is a complete verbal noun
Added dual possessive pronoun to show the conversation belongs to two people

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is سَمِيعٌۢ بَصِيرٌ)

Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَسْمَعُ - hears)

steps.m: Those who (referring to people who يُظٰهِرُونَ - do zihar)

Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: they declare (about their wives) (subject of ٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word refers to husbands making declarations about their wives, as mentioned in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added the plural 'you' pronoun to show who is being addressed

steps.n: This is a negative particle used here to deny that the wives they declare forbidden are actually their mothers. It's different from the conditional إِنْ (if) - here it means 'not' because it's negating a statement.

steps.m: to be a mother, to lead as a mother does

steps.n: The ُ (damma) sound makes it into the noun 'mother'
The ات ending makes it into a plural noun meaning 'mothers'
Adding attached pronoun meaning 'their'
The ُ shows this is in the subject/nominative position

steps.m: those who (feminine plural, referring to أُمَّهٰتِهِمْ - their mothers)

Added ونَ at the end to show many people are doing it together

steps.m: we sent down (referring to the divine revelations)
steps.n: Adding نَآ at the end shows 'we' did the action. In the Quran, when Allah uses 'We', it's the royal We showing majesty and power

Root letters combine to make a noun meaning 'one miraculous sign'
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form
Adds the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word is being described by what comes after it
The doubled letter makes the meaning stronger - very clear, definitive
Adding shadda (double letter) makes the meaning stronger
Adding ة makes it into a specific instance or concrete example
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs
Shows the word is affected by a preposition
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changes from 'he rejected' to 'one who rejects' by following the فَاعِل pattern - like changing 'write' to 'writer'
Adding the masculine plural suffix ين to indicate multiple disbelievers

steps.m: for the ones who reject truth

Added و (wa) meaning 'and' at the beginning of the word
Adding 'و' at the beginning means 'and', connecting this phrase to something that came before it.
This is the basic concept of punishment or torment
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding alif after the second root letter turns the verb into a noun describing the action
Adding tanween (double dammah) makes it 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'

steps.m: to humiliate or make something insignificant

steps.n: Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle letter to make an adjective meaning 'causing humiliation' (describes punishment)
Added tanween (double damma) at end to show it's indefinite and in nominative case
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و becomes silent with sukun.

steps.m: the Day (when Allah will resurrect them)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is affected by a verb that comes after it (يَبْعَثُهُمُ - He will resurrect them). It means this is specifically the Day when the resurrection will happen.

steps.m: to send forth or raise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and ُ at end

steps.m: he raises them up (subject is اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show who is being raised up. This word is connected to اللَّهُ (Allah) who is doing the raising.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the proper name of God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.

steps.m: to gather or bring things/people together
gathered/united (describes Allah raising them all on judgment day)
steps.n: Root 'gather' becomes 'all gathered together' by adding ي in the middle - like turning 'collect' into 'collected'

steps.m: all together (describes how Allah will raise them)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the action happens, like saying 'completely' or 'altogether' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda on ب makes the meaning more intense and thorough
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ to show this happens as a result of or after something else (in this case, after Allah resurrects them all)

steps.m: and then He will inform them thoroughly (about what they did)
steps.n: Added هُم meaning 'them' as the object. In this ayah, Allah is informing them about their deeds.

steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to create a relative phrase. This combination connects to the verb عَمِلُوٓا۟ (they did) to mean 'with/by what they did'

steps.m: to do, to act, to work

steps.m: they did/worked (subject of يُنَبِّئُهُم - He will inform them)
steps.n: Added وا with elongation (ـٓ) to show multiple people did the action. This word is what they will be informed about in the ayah.

steps.m: He (Allah) kept perfect count of it (referring to what they did)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows what was counted (their deeds mentioned in عَمِلُوٓا۟). The long alif (ىٰ) helps connect to the هُ smoothly.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Basic past tense verb meaning 'forgot'

Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the verse

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَبْعَثُ - He raises)

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of يَبْعَثُ - He raises)

steps.m: a witness (describing Allah in this ayah)

steps.n: This is a special connecting word that links يَعْلَمُ (He knows) with what follows. In this context, it means 'whatever/that which' rather than its question meaning 'what?'

steps.m: in (location word for ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ - the heavens)

steps.m: in (location word for ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ - the heavens)

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who has complete knowledge. This form is often used for Allah's attributes to show they are perfect and complete.
